_meta.description: Human-readable explanations for FORGE gate_reason codes written to SIGNALS.gate_reason in the tester journal DB. Keys MUST match the exact string FORGE writes — verified against ea/FORGE.mq5 JournalRecordSignal calls. Do NOT guess keys; always read the EA source first.

_patterns.warmup_*: Wildcard: FORGE prepends 'warmup_' to an internal reason string. Any gate_reason starting with warmup_ means the EA's indicator buffers are not yet ready at the start of a backtest or live session.
_patterns.open_group_*: Wildcard: FORGE tried to open a group but the broker rejected the order. The suffix indicates the reason: invalid_stops, rr_below_floor, bad_stoplimit_price, etc.

no_setup.label: No valid pattern on this bar
no_setup.explanation: Neither the BB Breakout nor the BB Bounce conditions were met. Price did not reach a Bollinger Band or show a qualifying breakout move — FORGE simply found nothing tradeable.

entry_quality_direction.label: Not enough bars moving in trade direction
entry_quality_direction.explanation: Too few consecutive M5 candles are moving in the intended trade direction. FORGE requires a minimum number of aligned bars before entry to avoid chasing a random spike.

entry_quality_direction_cap.label: Directional bias too strong — counter-move risk
entry_quality_direction_cap.explanation: The last N bars have all moved in the same direction — price may be overextended. FORGE caps stacked directional bars to avoid entering at the end of a one-sided move.

entry_quality_body.label: Candle body too small (indecision)
entry_quality_body.explanation: The entry candle's body (distance from open to close) is too small relative to its full range (high to low). A small body means the market is undecided — not a clean directional move FORGE wants to trade.

entry_quality_atr.label: ATR (volatility) too low at this bar
entry_quality_atr.explanation: The Average True Range — a measure of how much price moves each bar — is below the required minimum. When ATR is too small, the spread takes up too large a share of the potential profit.

entry_quality_atr_ext.label: ATR extension too narrow for viable trade
entry_quality_atr_ext.explanation: A secondary ATR check applied after the main setup is found. Even though a BB Breakout or Bounce was detected, the ATR at this specific bar is too small to reach the minimum TP1 target after spread costs.

entry_quality_bb_contraction.label: Bollinger Bands contracting — no momentum
entry_quality_bb_contraction.explanation: The Bollinger Bands are narrowing (squeezing together). A breakout setup requires the bands to be expanding, signalling increasing volatility. Contracting bands mean the market is coiling, not breaking out.

entry_quality_rsi_buy_ceil.label: RSI too high — overbought, do not buy
entry_quality_rsi_buy_ceil.explanation: The RSI is above the buy ceiling (default 70). Buying when RSI is overbought risks entering right at the top of a move, just as the market is most likely to reverse downward.

entry_quality_rsi_sell_floor.label: RSI too low — oversold, do not sell
entry_quality_rsi_sell_floor.explanation: The RSI is below the standard sell floor (default 33). Selling into an already oversold market risks triggering a Cardwell reversal — price snapping back sharply against the sell.

entry_quality_rsi_sell_adx_floor.label: RSI too low for sell — stricter floor because ADX is weak
entry_quality_rsi_sell_adx_floor.explanation: Same as entry_quality_rsi_sell_floor, but a higher (stricter) RSI floor is applied because ADX is below the threshold that defines a strong trend. In weak-trend conditions, oversold bounces are more likely, so FORGE raises the bar for entering a sell.

entry_quality_rsi_rising_sell.label: RSI rising into sell — losing bearish momentum
entry_quality_rsi_rising_sell.explanation: RSI is increasing on the sell signal bar. This means bearish momentum is fading even as the breakout fires. FORGE blocks the sell because the indicator trend and price direction are diverging.

entry_quality_adx_min_sell.label: ADX too low — not enough trend for a sell breakout
entry_quality_adx_min_sell.explanation: The Average Directional Index (ADX) measures trend strength, not direction. It is below the required minimum (default 20) for a sell breakout. Without enough trend, the breakout is more likely to fail and reverse.

entry_quality_adx_extreme_sell.label: ADX too high — trend may be overextended
entry_quality_adx_extreme_sell.explanation: ADX has surpassed the upper threshold. An extremely high ADX often signals the trend is near exhaustion and a reversal or consolidation is near. FORGE blocks new entries when the trend has been running too long.

entry_quality_adx_spike_sell.label: ADX spike on sell bar — momentum unreliable
entry_quality_adx_spike_sell.explanation: ADX jumped sharply on this specific bar. A sudden ADX spike can be caused by a news event, slippage, or a single large candle — not a genuine sustained trend. FORGE skips the entry until ADX stabilises.

entry_quality_macd_q0_bull_rising.label: MACD Q0 — bullish and rising (blocks sell)
entry_quality_macd_q0_bull_rising.explanation: The MACD histogram is above zero and increasing (Quadrant 0: Bull Strong). This is a bullish signal. FORGE blocks a sell breakout when MACD is pushing upward — the indicator and the trade would be pointing in opposite directions.

entry_quality_macd_q1_bull_fading.label: MACD Q1 — bullish but fading (blocks buy)
entry_quality_macd_q1_bull_fading.explanation: The MACD histogram is above zero but decreasing (Quadrant 1: Bull Fading). Upward momentum is waning. FORGE uses this to block buys that are chasing a move that may be ending.

entry_quality_macd_q2_bear_str.label: MACD Q2 — bearish and strengthening (blocks buy)
entry_quality_macd_q2_bear_str.explanation: The MACD histogram is below zero and falling (Quadrant 2: Bear Strong). Bearish momentum is accelerating. FORGE blocks a buy entry — buying into strengthening bearish momentum is high-risk.

entry_quality_macd_q3_bear_fading.label: MACD Q3 — bearish but recovering (ambiguous — blocks both)
entry_quality_macd_q3_bear_fading.explanation: The MACD histogram is below zero but rising (Quadrant 3: Bear Fading). The market is in a bearish-to-neutral transition. Direction is unclear, so FORGE blocks both aggressive buys and sells until MACD picks a side.

entry_quality_h1_di_buy.label: H1 DI+ not above DI- for buy
entry_quality_h1_di_buy.explanation: The 1-hour Directional Indicators show DI- (selling pressure) is still stronger than DI+ (buying pressure). A buy signal on M5 needs the hourly chart to confirm bullish direction before entry.

entry_quality_h1_di_sell.label: H1 DI- not above DI+ for sell
entry_quality_h1_di_sell.explanation: The 1-hour Directional Indicators show DI+ (buying pressure) is still stronger than DI- (selling pressure). A sell signal on M5 needs the hourly chart to confirm bearish direction before entry. Added in 2.7.12 to catch false breakdowns where H1 is still bullish. No ADX bypass (unlike the BUY counterpart which bypasses at counter_buy_adx_threshold).

entry_quality_h1_macd_sell.label: H1 MACD histogram still bullish — blocks sell
entry_quality_h1_macd_sell.explanation: The 1-hour MACD histogram is >= 0, meaning hourly momentum is not yet bearish. FORGE blocks the SELL until H1 momentum confirms the breakdown. Paired with entry_quality_h1_di_sell as the H1 confirmation pair. Added in 2.7.12.

entry_quality_h1_macd_buy.label: H1 MACD histogram already bearish — blocks buy
entry_quality_h1_macd_buy.explanation: The 1-hour MACD histogram is < 0, meaning hourly momentum is stalling even though DI may say the trend is still up. Classic trend-exhaustion BUY-the-top trap. Added in 2.7.17 after Run 15 G5002 lost -$400 with H1 trend=+2.139 (max bullish DI) but H1 MACD=-2.112 (momentum gone).

entry_quality_breakout_cooldown.label: BB_BREAKOUT same-direction cooldown — stacking onto fading momentum
entry_quality_breakout_cooldown.explanation: A prior BB_BREAKOUT entry in the same direction fired recently (within `same_dir_cooldown_seconds`, default 900s = 15 min). FORGE blocks consecutive same-direction breakouts to avoid the 'double-tap' failure mode where the second entry stacks onto fading momentum and stops out. Added in 2.7.17 after Run 15 G5002 lost -$400 firing 5 minutes after G5001's same-direction breakout.

entry_quality_hid_bull_div_sell.label: Hidden bullish RSI divergence — pullback about to reverse up
entry_quality_hid_bull_div_sell.explanation: HID_BULL = price made a higher low while RSI made a lower low — the classic Cardwell signature for a downtrend pullback about to reverse upward. FORGE blocks SELL when this divergence is active. Added in 2.7.13 after the Run 11 G5008 catastrophe lost -$940 with HID_BULL active at entry.

entry_quality_h4_rsi_sell_blocked.label: H4 RSI already oversold — avoid new sell
entry_quality_h4_rsi_sell_blocked.explanation: The 4-hour RSI is below its lower threshold. Selling here would be adding to a position on the 4-hour timeframe that is already deeply oversold. Higher-timeframe exhaustion increases reversal risk.

entry_quality_h4_adx_sell_blocked.label: H4 ADX too low — no H4 trend to support sell
entry_quality_h4_adx_sell_blocked.explanation: The 4-hour ADX is below the minimum required to confirm a sell on the higher timeframe. Without H4 trend alignment, an M5 breakout sell may be fighting against a ranging 4-hour market.

entry_quality_h4_rsi_buy_blocked.label: H4 RSI already overbought — avoid new buy
entry_quality_h4_rsi_buy_blocked.explanation: The 4-hour RSI is above its upper threshold. Buying here risks chasing an extended 4-hour move that is already overbought — a correction or reversal is more likely than continuation.

entry_quality_h4_adx_buy_blocked.label: H4 ADX too low — no H4 trend to support buy
entry_quality_h4_adx_buy_blocked.explanation: The 4-hour ADX does not confirm a bullish trend. An M5 buy breakout without H4 trend support is more likely to be a false breakout in a choppy 4-hour range.

entry_quality_m30_not_bearish.label: M30 not confirming bearish structure
entry_quality_m30_not_bearish.explanation: The 30-minute chart is not showing a bearish structure (falling highs, falling lows, or bearish EMA alignment). FORGE requires the M30 to agree with the M5 sell signal to reduce false breakouts.

entry_quality_session_sell_cutoff.label: Sell cutoff time — no new sells after this hour
entry_quality_session_sell_cutoff.explanation: New sell entries are blocked after a configurable hour (default NY 17:00 UTC, London 00:00 UTC). Holds avoid holding new short positions through thin overnight markets with unpredictable gaps.

entry_quality_news_filter.label: News blackout — high-impact event imminent or recent
entry_quality_news_filter.explanation: A high-impact economic event (e.g. Non-Farm Payrolls, CPI, FOMC) is within the blackout window. FORGE blocks new entries entirely around these events — volatility spikes make entries unpredictable and stops unreliable.

entry_quality_news_rsi_tighten.label: Near news event — entry criteria tightened
entry_quality_news_rsi_tighten.explanation: A medium-impact news event is approaching. Entries are not fully blocked but FORGE requires a tighter RSI range before allowing the trade. This signal failed the stricter criteria.

open_groups.label: Maximum concurrent positions reached
open_groups.explanation: FORGE already has the maximum number of trade groups open at the same time (default 2). No new positions can be opened until at least one existing group closes fully.

session_trade_cap.label: Maximum trades for this session reached
session_trade_cap.explanation: The configured cap on how many trades can be placed per session has been hit. This prevents over-trading within a single London or New York session regardless of how many signals appear.

session_off.label: Outside trading session
session_off.explanation: The current bar is outside the allowed trading hours. By default FORGE trades London and New York sessions only — the Asian session is excluded due to thin liquidity and unpredictable price action.

spread.label: Spread too wide — entry too expensive
spread.explanation: The bid-ask spread at this moment exceeds the configured maximum (default 30 points for XAUUSD). A wide spread makes the entry uneconomical — the cost to enter is too large relative to the expected profit.

rr_too_low.label: Risk:Reward ratio below minimum
rr_too_low.explanation: The distance from the entry price to TP1 is not large enough compared to the stop-loss distance. FORGE requires a minimum R:R ratio (default 1.5×) to ensure the potential reward justifies the risk on every trade.

cooldown.label: Post-loss cooldown — waiting before re-entry
cooldown.explanation: A trade recently hit its stop-loss. FORGE enforces a cooldown period (default 90 seconds) before allowing any new entry. This prevents immediately re-entering a losing trade in the same adverse conditions.

post_sl_cooldown.label: Extended cooldown after stop-loss — sell side
post_sl_cooldown.explanation: A sell group recently hit its stop-loss. An extended cooldown (default 3600 seconds = 1 hour) blocks new sells. This is a stricter version of the general cooldown applied specifically when a sell trade was stopped out.

direction_cooldown.label: Same-direction cooldown — too soon after last entry
direction_cooldown.explanation: A trade in the same direction (SELL or BUY) was opened very recently. A short bar-count cooldown is enforced to prevent stacking entries in rapid succession before the first position has had time to develop.

m1.label: M1 microstructure gate — short-term noise
m1.explanation: A 1-minute timeframe filter rejected the entry. Even when the M5 setup looks clean, this gate can detect that price action on the 1-minute chart is too choppy or counter-trend to justify the entry at that exact moment.

regime_countertrend.label: Regime engine — trading against the macro trend
regime_countertrend.explanation: The AURUM Regime Engine has classified the current market state (e.g. TREND_BEAR) and the proposed entry would trade against it. FORGE can be configured to block or reduce size on counter-trend signals when regime confidence is high.

execution_failed.label: Broker rejected the order
execution_failed.explanation: FORGE sent the entry order to the broker but the broker returned an error (e.g. invalid stops, requote, insufficient margin). The signal was valid but could not be executed. Check the EA Expert log for the specific broker error code.

warmup_tester_m5_rollovers.label: Backtest warmup — waiting for M5 bars to accumulate
warmup_tester_m5_rollovers.explanation: At the very start of a Strategy Tester backtest, the M5 indicator buffers (RSI, Bollinger Bands, ATR, ADX) do not have enough historical bars to produce reliable values. FORGE discards these early signals to avoid trades based on incomplete indicator data.

warmup_m5_bars.label: Warmup — not enough M5 bar history
warmup_m5_bars.explanation: The M5 timeframe does not yet have enough bars loaded for the indicators to calculate. Typically seen at the start of a session or after a terminal restart.

warmup_m15_bars.label: Warmup — not enough M15 bar history
warmup_m15_bars.explanation: The M15 (15-minute) timeframe does not yet have enough bars for the confirmation indicators. FORGE requires history across multiple timeframes before trading.

warmup_h1_bars.label: Warmup — not enough H1 bar history
warmup_h1_bars.explanation: The H1 (1-hour) trend timeframe does not yet have enough bars. H1 trend strength is required for entry decisions, so FORGE waits until enough hourly bars are available.

warmup_h4_bars.label: Warmup — not enough H4 bar history
warmup_h4_bars.explanation: The H4 (4-hour) timeframe does not have enough bars. H4 RSI and ADX gates require H4 history to compute.

open_group_rr_below_floor.label: Order rejected — R:R below the hard floor
open_group_rr_below_floor.explanation: After stop calculations, the actual risk:reward ratio of the order fell below the hard floor (a stricter minimum than the pre-check). Broker price or spread moved between the signal and the order placement, making the trade unviable.

open_group_invalid_stops.label: Order rejected — invalid stop levels
open_group_invalid_stops.explanation: The broker rejected the order because the stop-loss or take-profit price violated the broker's minimum stop distance rule. This can happen during fast markets when prices move between FORGE calculating the stops and sending the order.
